headline: Tennessee's ground game overwhelms Clemson in lopsided ACC opener
body: Tennessee's offense dictated the game from the outset, outgaining Clemson 599 to 348 total yards and converting the Tigers' lone turnover into points that would ultimately decide the outcome. The Volunteers' balance proved suffocating: 155 rushing yards and 444 passing yards gave Clemson's defense no single wrinkle to exploit, and Tennessee's defense capitalized when it mattered most, with defensive end Loftin recording two sacks that stalled critical Clemson drives in the third and fourth quarters.

James Cortez engineered an efficient night through the air for Clemson, completing 20 of 39 passes for 303 yards with two touchdowns and one interception. His receivers gave him legitimate options, particularly Fields, who hauled in six catches for 139 yards and a score. Yet Clemson's running back Adams Jr. managed just 16 yards on eight carries, starved of space by a Tennessee defense that never bent. That anemia on the ground proved fatal: when the Tigers needed to control clock and shorten the game late, they had nowhere to turn.

Tennessee's execution on both lines won this game. Running back Bishop punched in two touchdowns despite carrying just twelve times for 60 yards, a testimony to goal-line effectiveness. MacIntyre's composure as a passer—447 yards on 23 completions—and receiver Smith's 10-catch, 242-yard evening provided the depth that buried Clemson. The Volunteers' defense bent but never broke, and when Clemson had the ball with the game within reach, Loftin's rush presence turned opportunity into stagnation.
turningPoint: Tennessee's second sack by Loftin in the fourth quarter forced a Clemson three-and-out with under six minutes remaining and the Tigers trailing by eight, effectively sealing the outcome.

unsungHero.name: Loftin
unsungHero.why: His two sacks and constant interior pressure didn't show up in the box score the way MacIntyre's passing yards did, but they stymied Clemson's final scoring opportunities when the game was still contested.
